status: This corpus is repo-owned and rsbuild-adapted; this manifest records original upstream provenance only.
renamesNote: The `renames` map records upstream path -> corpus path for every `git mv` applied on top of the pinned ref (history preserved), plus the earlier collapse of the upstream vite-8-template into vite-7-template (which was then renamed to rsbuild-template). Upstream filenames/dirs saying 'vite' are misleading under rsbuild, so they were renamed to rsbuild-flavored names. In addition to these path renames, several exports inside integration/helpers/rsbuild.ts (formerly vite.ts) were renamed for accuracy: `viteConfig` -> `rsbuildConfig` (emits rsbuild.config.ts contents), `viteMajorTemplates` -> `bundlerTemplates`, `vitePreview` -> `rsbuildPreview`, `viteDevCmd` -> `rsbuildDevCmd`, plus internal type aliases `ViteConfig*Args` -> `RsbuildConfig*Args` and `FrameworkModeViteMajorTemplateName` -> `FrameworkModeBundlerTemplateName`. integration/helpers/templates.ts's `viteMajorTemplates` was likewise renamed to `bundlerTemplates`. All corpus call sites were updated. Describe/test titles saying 'Vite' were retitled to rsbuild-accurate names, except inside the two describe.skip'ed Vite-only suites (integration/plugin-order-validation-test.ts and integration/plugin-cloudflare-test.ts, whose skip reasons explain their Vite-only nature) and where a title names an actual asserted string or on-disk path ('Vite Environment API message' and 'Vite manifests' in integration/server-bundles-test.ts). RSC preview/framework fixtures still reference real @vitejs/plugin-rsc runtime packages and vite/client types; those are actual dependencies, not naming, and were left unchanged.
note: 13 checked-in corpus path(s) outside the adapter-owned overlay differ from a fresh sync of the pinned ref. They carry local Rsbuild adaptations; this sync run overwrote them in the working tree. Run `git checkout -- tests/react-router-framework` to restore them, or review `git diff` and upstream/formalize the changes.
